steps.title: Graph the useful candidates
steps.content: This is a model-fit question, so use the graph instead of solving symbolically. The points climb in roughly even steps, so a linear model is appropriate; the exponential choices would curve far too sharply for $x$-values in the thousands. In Desmos, plot a few visible data anchors and graph the two linear answer choices.

steps.title: Pick the right sign and intercept
steps.content: $y$ clearly increases as $x$ increases, so the slope must be positive. Choice B has slope $-1.2$, so it slopes the wrong way. Choice A, $y=-694+1.2x$, has positive slope and runs through the middle of the visible data cloud.

steps.title: Verify choice A against a data point
steps.content: Use the point near $(3200, 3100)$. Plugging into $y = -694 + 1.2x$: $$y = -694 + 1.2(3200) = -694 + 3840 = 3146,$$ very close to the observed $\approx 3100$. The fit is good. That matches choice A — A is correct.
